a: Forward Live Activity alerts from iPhone to an accessory you develop.
Responses to the Live Activity forwarding permission prompt.
An option that indicates the person allows Live Activity forwarding from all apps that support them.
An option that indicates the person doesn’t allow Live Activities.
An option that indicates the person allows Live Activities from a selected subset of apps.
An option that indicates the system hasn’t shown the authorization prompt to the person.
The details of the Live Activity that iOS forwards to your accessory.
The content of an alert for a forwarded Live Activity.
The content you use as an alert for a Live Activity update on your accessory.
An on-demand reference to the app icon of the app that started the Live Activity.
A class for requesting permission to forward Live Activities to your accessory and handle them in your accessory’s data provider extension.
A protocol that defines methods for handling Live Activity life cycle events in your accessory’s data provider extension.
An object that represents the active connection between your data provider extension and the system.

t: Receiving Live Activity updates and alerts on an accessory
a: Enable your accessory to receive forwarded Live Activities by updating your data provider extension.
Establishes communication between the data provider extension and the system.
A string that uniquely identifies the Live Activity.
Provides an updated Live Activity.
Provides an updated Live Activity and requests confirmation that the accessory displayed an alert.
The Live Activity update’s alert message.
Checks whether someone permits the system to forward Live Activities to the given accessory.
The body text of the alert.
The updated content of the forwarded Live Activity.
A constant you use to configure your data provider extension’s capability to forward Live Activities to your accessory.
Creates an object you use to manage Live Activity forwarding for your accessory.
The currently active Live Activities that the accessory is authorized to receive.
Delivers a message from the paired accessory to your data provider extension.
Presents the system UI that allows people to manage their permission to forward Live Activities to an accessory.
Sends a message to the paired accessory.
Indicates that the system invalidated the session and stopped sending Live Activity updates.
The icon of the app that initiated the Live Activity.
The bundle identifier of the app that started the Live Activity.
The name of the app from which the activity content originated.
The current state of the activity in its life cycle.
The title of the alert.
A URL that locates the icon file’s data.
